prompt: Build a RailOps Offline Schedule & Inventory Control Suite that lets a regional rail operator plan trips, manage seats and fares, and track onboard supplies with full auditability even when the dispatch office has no internet. The Vue.js web interface presents a role-based experience for Guest (read-only search), Host (station agent who publishes schedules and manages inventory moves), and Platform Operations (approver and auditor). Guests can search trips using combined filters for origin, destination, travel date (MM/DD/YYYY), and seat class, then sort by departure time, duration, or price; fuzzy matching handles typos and common station abbreviations, and the UI shows instant feedback like “No matches—try nearby dates” plus cached “hot searches” stored locally to keep repeated queries under 200 ms. Hosts create and edit schedules in a draft workspace, attach a pre-publish checklist that must pass before release (for example: at least one stop, valid time sequence, seat capacity between 1 and 500, price between $1.00 and $999.00, and no overlap on the same trainset). Before publishing, Hosts can compare versions side-by-side with highlighted diffs and request approval when required; Platform Operations can approve, reject with a mandatory comment, or trigger rollback to any prior published version, with the UI clearly labeling which version is active and when it took effect. In the same console, Hosts run inventory workflows for receiving, shipping, material return, customer return, and stock counts; alerts appear when on-hand drops below reorder points (default 20 units, configurable per item) or when count variance exceeds 2% or $50.00 in extended cost, with optional batch/serial capture for controlled items like radios or medical kits.The backend uses Koa to expose offline-first REST-style APIs to the Vue.js client over the local network with transport encryption enabled via TLS on internal endpoints, and MySQL persists all operational data, version history, and audit trails on-site. Authentication is strictly local username and password (no WeChat/OAuth, no SMS/email codes), with secure password storage using a strong salted hash (e.g., bcrypt cost factor 12), session tokens that auto-renew up to 8 hours of inactivity and hard-expire at 30 days, and multi-device controls that cap a user at 2 active sessions unless a Platform Operations user grants an exception. Risky-device verification is implemented offline by flagging new device fingerprints (browser + OS + machine identifier) and requiring a second-factor challenge based on pre-generated one-time recovery codes issued at enrollment and stored only as salted hashes. Sensitive-field masking applies in the UI and APIs (for example, only last 4 digits of any stored phone number; full values visible only to Platform Operations), and data access isolation enforces row-level scoping so Hosts see only their assigned stations while Platform Operations can cross-site audit. Rate limiting blocks brute-force attempts with progressive lockouts (5 failed logins in 10 minutes triggers a 15-minute lock; 3 lockouts in 24 hours requires admin reset). Backup and recovery run locally on a schedule (nightly full backup, 15-minute binlog increments) to an administrator-designated removable drive path, with quarterly restore drills supported in-app. Data quality and lineage are tracked for schedules and inventory events with completeness, uniqueness, and freshness checks at write-time and daily reporting; issues are logged with severity, owner, and due date, and point-in-time backtracking allows operators to replay a sample of transactions, diff two points in time, and document corrective actions without relying on any external services.
